Protein names: Methylcobamide:CoM methyltransferase MtaA (EC 2.1.1.246) (Methylcobalamin: Coenzyme M methyltransferase) (Methylcobamide:CoM methyltransferase II isozyme M) (MT2-M) ([Methyl-Co(III) methanol-specific corrinoid protein]:coenzyme M methyltransferase)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the uroporphyrinogen decarboxylase family. MtbA/mtaA subfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=methyl-Co(III)-[methanol-specific corrinoid protein] + coenzyme M = Co(I)-[methanol-specific corrinoid protein] + methyl-coenzyme M + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:45208, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:17570, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:17571, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:16379, ChEBI:CHEBI:58286, ChEBI:CHEBI:58319, ChEBI:CHEBI:60494; EC=2.1.1.246; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:8702528};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Methyltransferase involved in methanogenesis in the methanol pathway. Catalyzes the transfer of the methyl group from the methylated corrinoid protein MtaC to coenzyme M, forming the substrate for coenzyme-B sulfoethylthiotransferase. MtaC can be substituted by free cob(I)alamin in vitro. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:8702528}.
